title: niphogeton dissecta
intro: Niphogeton dissecta, also known as 'dissected water hemlock', is a plant species that belongs to the family Apiaceae. It is known for its toxic properties, but some of its parts have been used in herbal medicine, particularly for their anthelmintic properties, which help in expelling intestinal worms. The health benefits of this plant are primarily attributed to its ability to treat intestinal parasites and worms, making it a potential remedy for conditions like hookworm and roundworm infestations. Niphogeton dissecta is traditionally used to prepare herbal remedies such as infusions and decoctions, but its use should be approached with caution due to its potential toxicity.
